title: Fragment II - Sunset
content:
В В В В The day and its delights are done;
В В В В So all delights and days expire:
В В В В Down in the dim, sad West the sun
В В В В Is dying like a dying fire.

В В В В The fiercest lances of his light
В В В В Are spent; I watch him droop and die
В В В В Like a great king who falls in fight;
В В В В None dared the duel of his eye

В В В В Living, but, now his eye is dim,
В В В В The eyes of all may stare at him.

В В В В How lovely in his strength at morn
В В В В He orbed along the burning blue!
В В В В The blown gold of his flying hair
В В В В Was tangled in green-tressпїЅd trees,
В В В В And netted in the river sand
В В В В In gleaming links of amber clear;
В В В В But all his shining locks are shorn,
В В В В His brow of its bright crown is bare,
В В В В The golden sceptre leaves his hand,
В В В В And deeper, darker, grows the hue
В В В В Of the dim purple draperies
В В В В And cloudy banners round his bier.

В В В В O beautiful, rose-hearted dawn!
В В В В O splendid noon of gold and blue!
В В В В Is this wan glimmer all of you?
В В В В Where are the blush and bloom ye gave
В В В В To laughing land and smiling sea?
В В В В The swift lights that did flash and shiver
В В В В In diamond rain upon the river,
В В В В And set a star in each blue wave?
В В В В Where are the merry lights and shadows
В В В В That danced through wood and over lawn,
В В В В And flew across the dewy meadows
В В В В Like white nymphs chased by satyr lovers?
В В В В Faded and perished utterly.

В В В В All delicate and all rich colour
В В В В In flower and cloud, on lawn and lea,
В В В В On butterfly, and bird, and bee,
В В В В A little space and all are gone,
В В В В And darkness, like a raven, hovers
В В В В Above the death-bed of the day.
В В В В 
В В В В .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .В В В В В В В В  .
В В В В 
В В В В So, when the long, last night draws on,
В В В В And all the world grows ghastly gray,
В В В В We see our beautiful and brave
В В В В Wither, and watch with heavy sighs
В В В В The life-light dying in their eyes,
В В В В The love-light slowly fading out,
В В В В Leaving no faint hope in their place,
В В В В But only on each dear wan face
В В В В The shadow of a weary doubt,
В В В В The ashen pallor of the grave.

В В В В O gracious morn and golden noon!
В В В В With what fair dreams did ye depart,
В В В В Beloved so well and lost so soon!
В В В В I could not fold you to my breast:
В В В В I could not hide you in my heart;
В В В В I saw the watchers in the West,
В В В В Sad, shrouded shapes, with hands that wring
В В В В And phantom fingers beckoning!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Victor James Daley, titled "Fragment II - Sunset", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
